response.justification: The candidate is a versatile, craftsmanship-oriented senior developer with strong testing culture and some genuinely relevant high-throughput systems work, but he misses the role's central minimum qualification by a wide margin: professional C++ experience amounts to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015 (plus a thesis project), against a required 8 years, and C++ does not even appear in his skills list. His large-scale infrastructure exposure (~2 years of low-latency microservices in DLang, some Kubernetes and AWS CDK) is real but short of the 5-year bar, and his career shows no formal technical-leadership role owning platform strategy across teams. The profile is a strong full-stack/product engineer, not a C++ cloud-compute platform lead.
strengths: Low-latency, high-throughput distributed systems experience (microservices handling millions of HTTP requests/sec with async I/O, caching, fibers)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, and authored open-source test frameworks (Fluent-Asserts, Trial)
Proven mentoring and knowledge-sharing record via workshops, multi-day courses, refactoring dojos, and conference talks
concerns: Roughly 7 months of professional C++ versus the required 8 years; recent work is TypeScript/Node.js and DLang, and C++ is absent from his skills list
Large-scale infrastructure and cloud architecture depth falls short of the 5-year requirement; most of the career is web/product development
No formal technical-leadership role leading cross-team strategy, priorities, and deliverables; unexplained employment gaps in 2020 and early 2025
